qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ZIP extraction breakout writes files outside the intended scan directory
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding points to the same GuardDog ZIP extraction branch in guarddog/utils/archives.py and the same root cause described by the advisory: calling zip.extract() with os.path.join(target_directory, file) so the attacker-controlled member name influences the extraction base and defeats the built-in traversal cleanup.

selected_finding.title: ZIP extraction breakout writes files outside the intended scan directory

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding identifies the exact advisory root cause in GuardDog's ZIP extraction handling, and the copied PoC produced an attacker-controlled filesystem write outside the intended extraction directory under .judge.
evidence: The advisory names guarddog/utils/archives.py and shows the vulnerable call zip.extract(file, path=os.path.join(target_directory, file)).
zip_extraction_breakout/vulnerability_report.json identifies the same file, the same ZIP branch, and the same misuse of the zipfile API as the source of the breakout write.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC wrote reproduction/escaped/payload.txt/escaped/payload.txt outside the intended extraction root reproduction/workdir/out and printed verification_passed=true.
